text.title: Local LLM Ollama Core Rules
text.summary: Six testable rules for Ollama-based local LLM integrations.

rule.statement: Model size + quantisation fit the host VRAM with ≥1 GB headroom. e.g. llama3.1:8b-q4 needs ~5 GB; 70b-q4 needs 48 GB+.
rule.rationale: Oversize models page to disk and become unusable; capacity check prevents wasted pulls.

rule.statement: A Modelfile pins quantisation, system prompt, and template. Default `latest` tags drift across pulls.
rule.rationale: Pinning produces reproducible behaviour across hosts.

rule.statement: Health-probe the daemon (GET /api/tags) before each request batch. Daemon-down silently returns connection errors that look like timeouts.
rule.rationale: Early detection avoids confusion between model latency and daemon outage.

rule.statement: Use the OpenAI-compatible endpoint (/v1) so cloud-vs-local swap is a base_url change. Native API used only when a feature is missing in /v1.
rule.rationale: Endpoint portability keeps cloud fallback trivial.

rule.statement: A cloud fallback model is declared in the config. Local failure → cloud call with the same prompt; log the escalation.
rule.rationale: Local capacity / capability limits should not become user-visible failures.

rule.statement: For serious tasks use ≥q5_K_M quantisation. q4 is acceptable for quick demos; q4 quality on long-context tasks is sometimes >10pp below q5.
rule.rationale: Quantisation tradeoff is the silent quality regressor; pin it explicitly.

rule.statement: Preconditions hold → wire Ollama integration per procedure.
Frontier-reasoning task or undersized hardware → skip; use cloud.
